title: OpenClaw v2026 3 1 — Claude 4 6 & WebSocket Streaming
author: OpenClawNewsFactor
summary_bullets: OpenCloud just shipped version 2026.3.1 and it is running Claude 4.6 with realtime websocket streaming and the agent response speed has completely changed the game.

The latest OpenClaw release is the most significant update since the framework's launch integrating OpenAI websocket streaming alongside Claude 4.6's adaptive reasoning engine to deliver near instantaneous agent decision-making.
Previously, OpenClaw communicated with LLMS via standard REST API polling, which introduced significant roundtrip latency between each agentic step.
Version 2026.3.1 replaces this entirely with persistent websocket connections, meaning the agent now receives a continuous token stream while simultaneously executing shell commands in parallel.
Combined with Claude 4.6's Six's adaptive reasoning, which dynamically adjusts its internal chain of thought depth based on task complexity.
OpenClaw can now handle multi-step developer workflows like diagnosing a server error, pulling the relevant logs, and deploying a hot fix in a single fluid, low latency execution loop.
The update also ships native Kubernetes support, making it production deployable at scale for the first time.
Realtime streaming plus adaptive reasoning is the architecture that turns a toy agent into genuine infrastructure.
